text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a central repository. Each merge is automatically verified through a build process and a series of automated tests. This process is often orchestrated by a CI pipeline, which starts with an automated build to compile the code. Then, running tests catches issues early—like bugs or integration problems—before they affect other developers. By detecting problems immediately, teams can fix them quickly, maintaining a stable codebase. This approach saves time and reduces risk, making development smoother and more reliable.
